summary: Backend: All 18 pytest tests PASSED (auth, teams, players, matches, stats+summary, lineups, trainings/attendance, comms/gallery). Frontend: BLOCKED by webpack compile errors due to multiple non-existent icon imports from @phosphor-icons/react v2.1.10. Could not test any UI flow because login page never renders behind the 'Compiled with problems:' overlay.

minor.issue: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is invalid per spec; only allow_origin_regex='.*' makes it work. Tighten to explicit origin to be robust.

ui_bugs.issue: Imports `WhistleIcon` from @phosphor-icons/react which does not exist in v2.1.10. (Patched to alias MegaphoneIcon)
Imports `EnvelopePlusIcon` from @phosphor-icons/react which does not exist; webpack compile fails. Line 251.

Many icon imports likely missing in v2.1.10 (e.g., WhistleIcon, EnvelopePlusIcon, StrategyIcon, ChartLineUpIcon, GearSixIcon, ImagesSquareIcon, ChatsCircleIcon, PaperPlaneTiltIcon, PushPinIcon, etc.). Webpack only surfaces the first failing module. After fixing one, the next file errors.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot reach login/dashboard due to compile error overlay; UI testing fully blocked.

action_items: URGENT: Replace all non-existent phosphor icon imports across frontend. Audit every file under src/pages and src/components. Either upgrade @phosphor-icons/react to a version that includes Whistle/EnvelopePlus/Strategy/etc. (check latest 2.x), OR swap to existing icon names (e.g., Whistle->Megaphone, EnvelopePlus->Envelope, Strategy->Crosshair, ChartLineUp->ChartLine, GearSix->Gear, ImagesSquare->Images, ChatsCircle->ChatCircle, PaperPlaneTilt->PaperPlane, PushPin->Pushpin, CalendarBlank->Calendar, UsersThree->Users, SignOut->SignOutIcon? confirm via package).
Tighten CORS: use explicit allow_origins=[REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL origin] when allow_credentials=True (browsers ignore '*' with credentials).
After icon fixes, retest full frontend flow (login, register, navigation across 10 pages, lineup drag/drop, datavolley intermediate+advanced, analytics charts).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 634 lines; consider splitting auth/teams/matches/stats into separate routers for maintainability.

Register endpoint uses default role='head_coach' and accepts 'role' from client unchecked – allows any client to self-register as head_coach. Consider whitelist + only allow 'player' default for public registration.
DELETE endpoints return 200 on non-existent resource (no 404). Add existence check + 404.
GET /api/teams/{id} does not check membership – any authenticated user can read any team by id.
Match/player/stats/lineup endpoints don't verify the authenticated user belongs to the team_id. Authorization missing.
stats_summary counts q=='error' for ALL actions (including dig errors as 'errors'); fine but ensure UI maps as expected.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created several TEST_-prefixed teams, players, matches, stats, lineups, trainings, announcements, messages, gallery items. Not auto-cleaned.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green (18/18 pytest pass). Frontend currently fails to compile due to wrong phosphor icon names. After main agent fixes icon imports, run the same playwright smoke test (login -> nav each page -> lineup positions -> matches create btn -> analytics svg count). Sample console error shows webpack overlay with 'WhistleIcon was not found in @phosphor-icons/react'.
rca of the issue: Frontend imports icon names like WhistleIcon, EnvelopePlusIcon, StrategyIcon, ChartLineUpIcon, etc., from @phosphor-icons/react ^2.1.10. These specific names are not present in installed version's exports list (v2.1.10 exports include Acorn/AcornIcon, ArrowArcLeft/Icon, etc.). Webpack fails compilation with 'export X was not found' for the first offending file (Register.jsx -> WhistleIcon, then Roster.jsx -> EnvelopePlusIcon after partial fix). Mitigation: bulk replace icon names to ones present in the installed version, or upgrade the package. Reproduction: open / or /login on the preview URL, the webpack 'Compiled with problems:' overlay covers the page.
